From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Namibian football club

This article is about the Namibian club. For the Mexican club commonly known as "UNAM", see [Pumas UNAM](/wiki/Pumas_UNAM "Pumas UNAM").

Football club

UNAM FC

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | University of Namibia Football Club |
| Nickname | Clever Boys [[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1) |
| Founded | 1990s; 35 years ago (1990s) |
| Ground | [UNAM Stadium](/w/index.php?title=UNAM_Stadium&action=edit&redlink=1 "UNAM Stadium (page does not exist)"), [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek") |
| League | [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership") |
| 2024–25 | 6th |
|  | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | **[Home colours](/wiki/Kit_(association_football) "Kit (association football)")** | **[Away colours](/wiki/Away_colours "Away colours")** | | |
|  | |

**University of Namibia Football Club**, commonly known as **UNAM F.C.**, is a football club founded in the late 1990s by the [University of Namibia](/wiki/University_of_Namibia "University of Namibia") from Windhoek in Namibia.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The team, as well as the women's football team (UNAM Bokkies), plays home games at the UNAM Stadium (Unam Sports Ground) in the southwest of Windhoek.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=UNAM_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Players were originally exclusively recruited from the University of Namibia through the Unam home league.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In the 2002–03 season, UNAM FC was promoted from the Namibia Second Division to the Namibia First Division.

In August 2014, the club acquired the right to start in the highest Namibian league, the Namibia Premier League, from [Ramblers FC](/wiki/Ramblers_FC "Ramblers FC"). Thus, in the 2014/15 season, UNAM FC competed in the first division for the first time.[[9]](#cite_note-auto-9)

On 2 October 2014, Grand Master Printing owed by former UNAM student and businessman Eliaser Ndimulunde took over the vacant position of shirt sponsor for N$60,000 per season.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In February 2023, the club announced that MUMI would become the main shirt sponsor in a 3-year deal of N$30,000 per year.[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1)
